---
title: "3.7 Class Variables and Methods"
subtitle: "Class Creation"
format:
  clean-revealjs:
    slide-number: true
    css: unit3-shared.css
lang: zh-CN
---

## Course Directory

### Return to the course outline

[← Back to AP CSA / 返回课程目录](../../index.html)

## Topic Intro

### `static` belongs to the class

A <span class="term">static</span> member belongs to the class itself, not to one individual object.

::: {.tight-list}
- static method: called on the class
- static variable: shared by all objects of the class
- instance variable: each object has its own copy
:::

## Class Methods

### Static methods are called with the class name

![Calling methods](../../assets/3-class-creation/3-7-class-variables-and-methods/FlowChartCallingMethods.png){fig-align="center" width="55%"}

```java
double root = Math.sqrt(25);
```

`Math.sqrt` is a static method. You do not create a `Math` object before calling it.

## Static Method Example

### No instance variables required

```java
public class Temperature
{
    public static double celsiusToFahrenheit(double celsius)
    {
        return celsius * 9.0 / 5.0 + 32;
    }
}
```

Call:

```java
double f = Temperature.celsiusToFahrenheit(20.0);
```

Static methods should not depend on one object's instance state.

## Class Variables

### One shared value for the whole class

```java
public class Person
{
    private static int personCount = 0;
    private String name;

    public Person(String personName)
    {
        name = personName;
        personCount++;
    }

    public static int getPersonCount()
    {
        return personCount;
    }
}
```

Every constructor call updates the same shared counter.

## Quick Check

### Trace the static counter

```java
Person a = new Person("Ana");
Person b = new Person("Bo");
Person c = new Person("Cam");
System.out.println(Person.getPersonCount());
```

Answer:

```text
3
```

There is one `personCount` for the class, not one per object.

## Debug Task

### Static method cannot use instance state directly

Problem:

```java
public static void printName()
{
    System.out.println(name);
}
```

`name` is an instance variable. A static method has no specific object.

Fix: make the method an instance method, or pass needed data as a parameter.

## `final` Keyword

### Constants should not change

```java
public static final double PI = 3.14159;
```

`final` means the value cannot be reassigned after initialization.

Class constants are often:

::: {.tight-list}
- `public`
- `static`
- `final`
- named with uppercase words
:::

## Groupwork Coding Challenge

### Static song and counter

Design a class that tracks how many song objects have been created.

```java
public class Song
{
    private static int count = 0;
    private String title;

    public Song(String songTitle)
    {
        title = songTitle;
        count++;
    }

    public static int getCount()
    {
        return count;
    }
}
```

Add one instance method that prints the song title.

## Classroom Check

### A complete answer should...

::: {.tight-list}
- distinguish static members from instance members
- call a static method with the class name
- explain why a static variable is shared by all objects
- trace a static counter across multiple constructor calls
- explain why `final` is used for constants
:::

## End

### Return to the course outline

[← Back to AP CSA / 返回课程目录](../../index.html)
